titlestmt.titleproper: "Conserving the Yale Center for British Art" film recordings by Zach Bjork
"Conserving the Yale Center for British Art" film recordings by Zach Bjork
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Meg Rinn, YCBA Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 20, 2025 at 18:00

did.unittitle: "Conserving the Yale Center for British Art" film recordings by Zach Bjork

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Archives
Yale Center for British Art. Communications and Marketing Department

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Yale Center for British Art, an iconic modern building in New Haven, Connecticut, designed by the American architect Louis I. Kahn to house Paul Mellon's extraordinary gift to Yale University, reopened to the public on May 11, 2016, after the completion of the third phase of a multi-year building conservation project.

Led by Knight Architecture, LLC, this was a complex building conservation project, comprising the entire structure, from roof to basement. It renewed the Center's public galleries, internal systems, spaces, and amenities, and provided an opportunity to reimagine and reinstall the Center's renowned collections of more than five centuries of British art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection of recordings by Zack Bjork consists of the interviews, footage, music, and the final twelve-minute film he created in conjunction with museum staff in 2016 about the conservation of the Yale Center for British Art. The documentary provides insight into the architecture of the museum's iconic building designed by Louis I. Kahn, the building conservation project completed in 2016, and the relationship of the building to the museum's permanent collections.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is unprocessed and may not be available for immediate access.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: "Conserving the Yale Center for British Art" film recordings by Zach Bjork. Yale Center for British Art, Archives.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are protected by copyright and/or related rights.

Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Materials for which copyright has been transferred to Yale may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact institutional.archives@yale.edu. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The final film can be viewed on YouTube at: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9Q1uWMP9eF0&t=609s

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into two series: Series I. Final Film; Series II. Production.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the final cut of the film.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into four subseries: subseries 1, archival footage; subseries 2, complete footage; subseries 3, interviews; subseries 4, music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the production compontents used to create the final film, including interviews, archival footage, music, and completed individual clips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains color footage of Paul Mellon, Jules David Prown, and Kingman Brewster walking through the museum during construction, along with improved audio track for the footage. The footage itself originates from the Offce of Public Affairs, Yale University, Motion Pictures (RU 840). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this subseries is arranged alphabetically by last name of the interview subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These six interviews, ranging from fifteen minutes to over an hour, feature current and former museum staff discussing the YCBA collection and building.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains two versions of the music composed for the film, DRY and REVERB. The REVERB group contains the same score as the DRY group, but with additional reverberation and enhancement.
